subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – The Cherry Tree
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: Rakesh lived with his grandfather in a small house near Mussoorie in the Himalayan foothills. His parents lived in a village where there was no school, so they sent him to live with his grandfather. Rakesh enjoyed the peaceful surroundings of the mountains and forests.
questions: Why did his parents send him to live there?
What did Rakesh enjoy about the place?

passages.paragraph: One day, while returning from the bazaar, Rakesh bought a handful of cherries. He ate most of them happily as he walked home. When he reached the garden, he kept the last cherry seed and decided to plant it in the soft soil.
questions: What did he do while walking home?
What did he keep in the end?
Where did he plant the seed?

passages.paragraph: Rakesh forgot about the seed for several days. Then one morning he noticed a small green shoot growing from the soil. The tiny plant was only a few inches high, but it made Rakesh very happy.
questions: What did he notice one morning?
How did Rakesh feel when he saw it?

passages.paragraph: Rakesh began to care for the young plant. He watered it regularly and placed small pebbles around it so that it would not be stepped on. Watching the plant grow slowly became one of his favorite activities.
questions: What did Rakesh begin to do?
How did he take care of the plant?
Why did he place pebbles around it?

passages.paragraph: One afternoon, a goat entered the garden and ate all the leaves of the young cherry tree. Only a thin stem remained. Rakesh felt sad when he saw the damaged plant.
questions: What did the goat do to the plant?
What part of the plant remained?
What had happened to the tree?

passages.paragraph: Some time later, a woman cutting grass accidentally cut the plant in two with her scythe. Despite this damage, the little tree did not die. After some weeks, new shoots began to grow again.
As the seasons passed, the cherry tree grew taller and stronger. Insects and birds began to visit it. One day, Rakesh saw a bright green praying mantis sitting quietly on a leaf.
questions: What creatures began to visit the tree?

passages.paragraph: During the monsoon rains, the tree grew quickly. Its leaves became thick and shiny. Rakesh was amazed at how strong the tree had become despite the many difficulties it had faced.
questions: Which season helped the tree grow quickly?
What difficulties had the tree faced?

passages.paragraph: After a few years, the cherry tree produced its first blossoms. The pale pink flowers looked beautiful in the sunlight. Rakesh and his grandfather stood quietly and admired the blossoms together.
questions: What appeared on the tree after a few years?

passages.paragraph: Later, the tree produced small cherries. Rakesh tasted one but found it sour. However, the birds loved the cherries and often came to eat them. Rakesh felt proud because he had grown the tree himself.
questions: What fruit did the tree produce?
What did Rakesh think of the taste?
Why did birds visit the tree?
